summary: New York City police arrested a man in connection with two stabbings on Manhattan’s Upper West Side Thursday afternoon and are evaluating whether the attacks amount to hate crimes, Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch said.

summary: Scribe Therapeutics Inc., a clinical-stage developer of gene therapies for heart diseases, raised $128.7 million in an upsized initial public offering priced at the top of its marketed range.

title: Why Nvidia’s Huang Pledged $75 Million to Fund Vanderbilt in San Francisco

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 24
summary: Brent crude traded near $100 a barrel after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea opened a new front in the Middle East conflict, while President Donald Trump threatened to extend US strikes on Iran.

title: Stock Slide Extends on AI Jitters, Oil Above $100: Markets Wrap
summary: A global selloff in technology stocks gained momentum as doubts over returns from billions of dollars spent on artificial intelligence were compounded by a surge in oil prices that rekindled inflation concerns.

title: Japan’s Katayama Says to Take Bold FX Steps Decisively as Needed
summary: Japanese authorities will take bold steps to counter moves in the currency market decisively as needed, and a US Treasury report issued overnight underscores a shared view that excessive currency moves are undesirable, Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said.

title: Gold Extends Decline as Higher Energy Costs Raise Rate-Hike Bets
summary: Gold extended a retreat as the widening conflict in the Middle East lifted energy prices and increased expectations the US Federal Reserve will tighten monetary policy to contain inflation.

title: PBOC Adds Most Liquidity to Economy in Five Months to Aid Growth
summary: China’s central bank made its biggest addition of liquidity to the economy in five months via its medium-term lending facility to support growth ahead of its late July Politburo meeting and to accommodate government bond issuance.

title: Korean Stocks Sink as Chipmakers Drop on Middle East, China IPO
summary: South Korean stocks fell as funds cut exposure to chipmakers after overnight losses in US peers, while factoring in a Chinese rival’s imminent share sale and rising Middle East tensions.

title: Metals Fluctuate as Middle East Escalation Keep Bulls in Check
summary: Base metals fluctuated at the end of the week as the threat of an escalating conflict in the Middle East — and more turbulence for the global economy — kept a lid on optimism.

title: India’s Markets Shrug Off Student Protests for Now
summary: Student protests in India’s capital, a political headache for Prime Minister Narendra Modi, aren’t reflecting in the country’s financial markets.

title: India May Allow Portfolio Managers to Short Sell Stocks
summary: India’s securities regulator has proposed allowing domestic portfolio management service providers to take short positions in equity derivatives for the first time, easing rules for professional money managers even as it tightens oversight of retail trading.

title: Wheat Extends July Rally as War Risks Stoke Fears Over Supply
summary: Wheat resumed its rally after a brief bout of profit-taking, as escalating geopolitical tensions and worsening weather in key producing regions continued to stoke supply concerns.

title: PHP/USD: Philippine Peso Falls to Fresh Record Low as Oil Advances
summary: The Philippine peso fell to a fresh record low as oil prices climbed, boosting pressure on the central bank to intervene as markets eye 62 as the next key level.

title: Malaysia Sells First Dollar Bonds in Five Years as Fuel Subsidy Bill Rises
summary: Malaysia raised $1.5 billion in its first dollar bond sale in five years, bolstering funding as the Southeast Asian nation grapples with a fuel subsidy bill that’s likely to more than double from an initial goal due to the Iran war.

title: India’s SEBI Mulls Letting Portfolio Managers Invest Clients’ Money Overseas
summary: SEBI proposes allowing investment in overseas securities and IPO-bound firms, among others.

title: CXMT’s Frenzy Ignites Hopes of Futher Hardware Rally in China
summary: Investors are looking to Monday’s trading debut of CXMT Corp. to help renew a rally in Chinese semiconductor stocks that has been on pause ahead of the memory giant’s initial public offering.

title: Manipal Health Seeks to Raise Up to $960 Million in India IPO
summary: Manipal Health Enterprises Ltd. is seeking to raise as much as 92.7 billion rupees ($960 million) in an initial public offering, continuing a run of sizable share sales in India.

title: China’s Gold Imports Surge After International Prices Slump
summary: Chinese gold imports rose to a two-year high in June, underscoring resilient demand in the world’s biggest bullion market after a plunge in international prices.

title: Chinese Stock Turnover Shrinks as Sentiment Cools on Tech Sector
summary: Chinese daily stock turnover dropped to the lowest level in over three months, as investors shun the nation’s technology sector.

title: Trump to Take Up Manila’s Concerns With Xi, Philippines Says
summary: US President Donald Trump assured Philippine leader Ferdinand Marcos Jr. that he would take up Manila’s concerns when he meets with Chinese President Xi Jinping, the Southeast Asian nation said.

title: Macquarie’s First Woman CEO Departs With a $339 Million Stake
summary: Shemara Wikramanayake is stepping down as head of Macquarie Group Ltd. after leading the Australian financial conglomerate to bumper profits and a record high share price. For that she will be richly rewarded, making her one of the best-paid female bank executives in the world.

title: Modi Seeks to Calm Anger in Rare Video as India Protests Widen
summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi made a fresh bid to calm mounting anger over examination paper leaks as students prepared nationwide protests on Friday, with demands growing for the education minister to step down.
